schemes.name: AFFDF-Financial Assistance For Treatment Of Serious Diseases To Non Pensioner Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)/Widows
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance to a non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen of all ranks and widows to meet medical expenses related to treatment of approved serious diseases like cancer, renal failure, knee replacement and heart surgery.

schemes.name: Atal Mission For Rejuvenation And Urban Transformation
schemes.description: Providing basic services (e.g. water supply, sewerage, urban transport) to households and building amenities in cities which will improve the quality of life for all, especially the poor and the disadvantaged is a national priority.

schemes.name: AVYAY - National Action Plan for Senior Citizens: Health and Shelter for Senior Citizens
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: The scheme aims to improve the quality of life of Senior Citizens, particularly those who are indigent, by providing basic amenities like shelter, food, medical care and entertainment opportunities. The scheme provides financial assistance for projects such as maintenance of Senior Citizen Homes.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH and Public Health
schemes.description: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH-based interventions to improve community health outcomes by supporting preventive, promotive, curative, and rehabilitative healthcare services.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH for Sports Medicine
schemes.description: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH interventions for the treatment and rehabilitation of sports injuries and to enhance health and fitness among sports persons.

schemes.name: Ayurswasthya Yojana: Up-gradation of Facilities to Centre of Excellence (CoE)
schemes.description: The scheme-component aims to support the establishment and up-gradation of reputed institutions to Centres of Excellence for strengthening AYUSH education, research, innovation, and advanced healthcare services.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
schemes.description: AB PM-JAY is a health insurance scheme for low-income families in rural and urban areas. The scheme aims to provide affordable healthcare facilities to the Poor. It is the largest health assurance scheme in the world which aims at providing a health cover of â¹5,00,000/- per family per year.
schemes.income_limit: Landless households deriving a major part of their income from manual casual labour - Automatically includedHouseholds without shelter 1.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance For Procuring Mobility Equipment To Disabled Ex-Servicemen (All Ranks)
schemes.description: The scheme provides financial assistance of a maximum of â¹ 1,00,000 from the Armed Forces Flag Day Fund (AFFDF) for the procurement of modified scooters, crutches and wheelchairs for Ex-Servicemen who are disabled after their retirement from service with a disability of 50% or more.

schemes.name: Fund of Funds for Startups
schemes.description: Launched in January 2016 under Startup India, the Fund of Funds for Startups is managed by SIDBI. It supports DPIIT-recognized startups indirectly by investing in SEBI-registered AIFs, which must invest at least twice the FFS contribution.

schemes.name: Health Ministerâs Discretionary Grant
schemes.description: A health scheme by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for financially poor patients to defray a part of the expenditure on Hospitalization/treatment in Government Hospitals,for life threatening diseases covered under Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN), in cases where free medical facilities aren't there.
schemes.income_limit: The Total Annual Family Income of the applicant must not exceed â¹1,00,000/- per annum.

schemes.name: Indian Community Welfare Fund
schemes.description: A scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for the welfare of Overseas Indians; assisting them in distress situations. ICWF has also been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, countries affected by natural disasters and other challenging situations.

schemes.name: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram
schemes.description: A scheme by Ministry of MoHFW for pregnant women who access government health facilities for their delivery. to absolutely free and no expense delivery, including caesarean section. Essential care is provided to the mother and her neonate within 48 hours.

schemes.name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
schemes.description: The scheme aims to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional deliveries among poor pregnant women. It offers cash assistance for institutional and home deliveries, along with antenatal and postnatal care support, primarily benefiting poor pregnant women across India.

schemes.name: National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) - "Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) Formation and Functionalisation"
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: The scheme aims to establish professional, well-trained, motivated and adequately equipped workforce for maintenance and management of sewers and septic tanks, addressing the critical need to eliminate fatalities resulting from workers entering sewers and septic tanks without proper safety measures.

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission: Establishment of State Semen Bank
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry of Fisheries,Animal Husbandry and Dairying
schemes.description: "Establishment of State Semen Bank (Genetic Improvement of Sheep and Goat breeds)" aims at the genetic improvement of sheep/goat breeds through selective breeding by propagation of superior male germplasm through artificial insemination.

schemes.name: National Youth Award
schemes.description: This award is to motivate young persons to achieve excellence in the field of national development or social service and to develop in them a sense of responsibility to the community and thus to improve their potential as good citizens.

schemes.name: Nikshay Poshan Yojana
schemes.description: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India has announced the scheme for incentives for nutritional support to TB patients. This scheme will be called âNikshay Poshan Yojanaâ.

schemes.name: Niramaya Health Insurance Scheme
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: Niramaya Health Insurance Scheme ,launched by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, aims to provide affordable health insurance to the Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).

schemes.name: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)
schemes.description: The scheme aims to improve the socio-economic status of PVTGs by saturating families and habitations with basic facilities and services. It provides 11 critical infrastructure and welfare interventions, including housing, water, electricity, and connectivity.

schemes.name: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
schemes.description: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government â aided schools. The Scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Education.

schemes.name: PM-Special Training of Geriatric Caregivers
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: The scheme aims to train and create a professional cadre of geriatric caregivers to meet the increasing demand for specialized care for senior citizens. It offers skill development and certification in geriatric care job roles to Indian citizens, ensuring professional services for the elderly.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana was launched by the Department of Pharmaceuticals, in November 2008. Under the scheme, dedicated outlets known as Janaushadhi Kendras are opened to make quality generic medicines available to all at affordable prices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Package : Insurance Scheme For Health Workers Fighting COVID-19
schemes.description: The "Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Package: Insurance Scheme for Health Workers Fighting COVID-19" by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India aims to offer financial protection to healthcare workers involved in COVID-19 duties.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: The scheme is to provide maternity benefits to women belonging to socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society. For first child the maternity benefit of â¹5,000 is provided in two instalments under PMMVY and second child, it is â¹6,000 is to be provided subject to second child is girl.
schemes.income_limit: Women whose net family income is less than Rs.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan
schemes.description: The scheme aims to provide fixed-day assured, comprehensive and quality antenatal care universally to all pregnant women in 2nd and 3rd trimester on the 9th of every month. It offers free comprehensive antenatal care services at government health facilities.

schemes.name: Rastriya Arogya Nidhi - Health Ministerâs Cancer Patient Fund
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance to poor patients living below poverty line and suffering from cancer, for their treatment at 27 Regional cancer centers (RCCs).

schemes.name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Medical Treatment Of Ex-Servicemen
schemes.description: A scheme to provide financial assistance to a non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen/widows of ranks up to Havildar/equivalent to meet routine medical expenses.

schemes.name: Scheme For Adolescent Girls
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.description: A support scheme by Ministry of Women & Child Development enabling self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls, and improving their nutritional, health & hygiene  status. All out-of-school adolescent girls in the 11 to 14 years age group are eligible for this scheme.

schemes.name: Scheme for Containing Population Decline of Small Minority Communities
schemes.description: The objective of the scheme is to reverse the declining trend of Parsi population by adopting a scientific protocol and structured interventions, stabilize their population and increase the population of Parsis in India.
schemes.income_limit: (ii) For Medical component annual family income should be less than Rs 30 lakh.

schemes.name: Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise (SMILE): Composite Medical Health for Transgender Persons
schemes.nodal_ministry: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.description: The scheme aims to provide health Insurance coverage to all Transgender persons living in India to improve their health condition through proper treatment including sex re-assignment surgeries as well as medical support. The scheme provides health insurance in the form of Ayushman Bharat Plus.

schemes.name: Surakshit Matritva Aashwasan Yojana
schemes.description: SUMAN Yojana is a maternity benefit initiative by MoH&FW. This program provides affordable and quality healthcare solutions to pregnant women and newborns. The scheme provides zero expense access up to six months after delivery. Upto antenatal check-ups during the 1st trimester are provided.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II
schemes.description: A sanitation scheme for Visual cleanliness through Open Defecation Free (ODF) sustainability and effective management of solid and liquid waste in villages.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission â Grameen PHASE I
schemes.description: The scheme aims to eliminate open defecation and improve rural sanitation by supporting toilet construction and promoting usage among poor and marginalized rural households.

schemes.name: Swachh Bharat Mission â Urban 2.0
schemes.description: A sanitation scheme for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL) for urban households.
